name: Elk OS — repackage the whole system as a self-host product (recursive test case)
description: **EPIC COMPLETE 2026-06-29 — all 8 phases green, live demo up.** Elk OS: the whole Analog Elk system repackaged as a one-command self-host product, built as the system's own final test case (Max Mode fan-out, the CMS as spine, adversarial verify, the human↔agent loop — the system shipping the system). P0 scaffold · P1 core · P2 schema+seed (built BY Max Mode) · P3 RAG · P4 portal · P5 Claude-OS wiring (loop proven) · P6 LIVE DEMO https://34.220.64.149.sslip.io · P7 packaging. Repo ~/Desktop/DevProd/elk-os (local main, ~25 commits). Full narrative: elk-os/WHITEPAPER-LOG.md (Sessions 0-7). Open follow-ups remain (GH repo+CI, portal branding/amd64 image, hardening, KB ingest) — productization polish, not blockers. Original ask was a whitepaper of the admin panel; it became: build the whole system as a sellable product AND make that the whitepaper's centerpiece.

name: Elk OS — create GitHub repo + activate CI/release-please/publish-images
description: **Repo CREATED + pushed 2026-06-30: https://github.com/AnalogElk/muster** (public, default branch main per §8, topics set, homepage=demo URL, CMS repositories row linked to project). Pushing triggered release-please (a v0.1.0 release PR should appear). Landing GitHub links updated -> real repo. REMAINING: (1) merge the release-please PR to cut v0.1.0 + tag; (2) portal-image publish job needs AE_FRONTEND_REPO + AE_FRONTEND_PAT repo secrets (private front-end source) — RAG image publishes without them; (3) wire the CI clean-room doctor gate (task 6fc19588). Branch protection (require PR) is ON — future changes via PR.
acceptance_criteria:
- Create the GitHub repo (e.g. AnalogElk/elk-os), default branch = main per §8, push the local history.
- That activates `.github/workflows/{publish-images,release-please}.yml`: first release-please PR cuts v0.1.0; tag publishes the RAG image (and portal image once AE_FRONTEND_REPO/PAT secrets are set) to GHCR.
- Wire the CI clean-room `doctor` gate (task 6fc19588) to run on PRs.
- Confirms the P7 deferred criteria (published images + CI green-install gate).

name: Muster — project-scoped queryable KB in the portal (kb_pages + bundled RAG /query + auto-reingest)
description: From Mike's sidenote while wiring the GitHub repo: a queryable per-project KB in the portal, with a process to keep it updated as features are added. Natural fit — Muster ships the RAG engine already. Distinct from the immediate KB fix (re-add pruned kb_* collections so the existing section works).
acceptance_criteria:
Roadmap (Mike's sidenote). Muster ALREADY bundles the RAG engine (P3) + has a portal KB (kb_spaces/kb_pages) — unify them:
1. Make kb_pages **project-scoped** (kb_page -> project m2o) so each project has its own knowledge.
2. **Auto-ingest** kb_pages into the bundled RAG engine (a Directus Flow on kb_page create/update -> POST /ingest), so the KB is semantically queryable.
3. A portal **search/query box** on the KB section that hits the RAG `/query` endpoint and returns cited kb_pages.
4. **Update process as features ship:** shipping a feature => write/update its kb_page (part of the CLOSE TASK ceremony); the Flow re-ingests automatically, so the KB stays fresh and queryable. Document this as the standing 'how to update the KB' loop.
This makes the portal's KB the human-authored source of truth + the RAG engine the query layer — coherent with the whitepaper's 'knowledge substrate'.

name: Muster — bake demo-box operational state into provisioning (reproducibility)
description: Flagged by the rebrand agent: 4 box-side changes (demo user, is_test_data flips, directus_users fields, PORTAL_IMAGE bump) are live operational state, not committed. Filed per §1.
acceptance_criteria: The working demo box has operational state that is NOT in the repo, so a fresh box won't reproduce it. Bake into elk-os provisioning/seed: (1) the demo user (demo@muster.dev) + its role; (2) the seed rows' is_test_data=false on a standalone demo box (or a demo profile that seeds visible content); (3) the two missing directus_users fields (is_test_data, test_only) added to the self-host schema snapshot; (4) the PORTAL_IMAGE pin in .env. So `provision/cloud-init` -> a working Muster demo with login, reproducibly.

name: P3 — RAG knowledge engine integration
description:
Bring the local 'Claude is the LLM' RAG engine into the stack.

**DONE & verified green 2026-06-29.** elk-v3 engine vendored into `rag-engine/` (source commit 02a38e5e, PROVENANCE.md), added as additive port-isolated overlay `compose/compose.rag*.yaml` (elkos-rag-postgres 15433 / qdrant 16333 / redis 16379 / rag-api 9100). Local `bge-small` embeddings baked into the image — NO external inference/key. `ELK_OS_WITH_RAG` (default on) bundles it into up/down/logs; `doctor` adds a RAG `/health` row honoring the degraded(WARN)-vs-down(red) signal. Booted all 4 containers healthy, `/health` ok. Found+fixed a real set -e/pipefail bug in doctor. Commits 4f5f5ad, 3ff37b0. Verified on disk by orchestrator.
acceptance_criteria:
- elk-v3 engine compose merged in (Postgres/Qdrant/Redis/RAG API on :9100).
- `doctor` checks RAG `/health` including the vector-health degraded signal.
- KB manifest ingest runs Claude-free (cron-safe); degrades to FTS if local embeddings unavailable.

name: P0 — Scaffold elk-os repo, CLI skeleton, secrets/profile layer, doctor framework
description:
Scaffold + resumable phased CLI skeleton + profile/secrets layer + doctor framework.

**DONE & verified 2026-06-29.** Repo `~/Desktop/DevProd/elk-os` on `main`; `bin/elk-os` executable with init/up/down/doctor/migrate/seed/wire/logs/help; structure, `.env` contract, profiles, design spec, and `WHITEPAPER-LOG.md` committed (6da7d6b + feat commits). No secrets committed. Verified on disk by orchestrator.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repo created with the agreed structure (bin/, compose/, profiles/{generic,analogelk}/, directus/, claude-os/, provision/, deploy/, docs/). - `bin/elk-os` runs and prints subcommand help (init/up/migrate/seed/wire/doctor/down). - `elk-os init --profile

` writes `.env` from `.env.example` with auto-generated secrets (DB pw, Directus admin pw, static token) and the chosen profile; never echoes secrets to stdout (§2). - `doctor` framework runs and prints a per-subsystem green/red board (checks may be stubs at P0). - Design doc + `WHITEPAPER-LOG.md` committed; first git commit on `main`.

name: P4 — Portal image build/publish + wired to fresh Directus behind Caddy
description:
The human surface, containerized and wired.

**DONE & verified green 2026-06-29.** Next.js 16 portal built §9-safely from a READ-ONLY git archive of pinned analog-elk-front-end origin/main (9931464) — the source repo (28 active worktrees) untouched. `elk-os/portal:local` (413MB, Next standalone) builds; `elk-os-portal` healthy; `/login` + `/` both HTTP 200; `doctor` Portal-HTTP row green; gated by ELK_OS_WITH_PORTAL. Agent cleared 5 real blockers (private GH-Packages dep stripped; output:standalone injected; instrumentation token-timing crash-loop -> up recreates portal post-mint; node healthcheck probe; compose context path). §5 screenshot attached (login page; tagline literally 'The agency OS that runs itself'). Commits c3adb38..15e99f7. KNOWN GAP: portal still shows Analog Elk branding on the generic profile (branding not yet profile-switched) — follow-up filed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Next.js portal built and published as a versioned Docker image (CI).
- Compose runs it wired to fresh Directus (DIRECTUS_URL + token + profile USE_STATIC_FALLBACK).
- Reachable behind Caddy; `doctor` asserts portal HTTP 200 on a key route.
- **Screenshot attached to the PR** per CLAUDE.md §5 before merge.

name: Elk OS — CI clean-room `doctor` gate (green-install release gate)
description: Spec §6 release gate. Flagged by the P0/P1 build agent as a real follow-up with no task id — filed here per CLAUDE.md §1 rather than left floating. Depends on the GitHub remote existing (created at/with P7).
acceptance_criteria:
- GitHub Actions workflow brings the whole compose up on the `generic` profile from zero and runs `elk-os doctor`, asserting green (Directus health + token + later RAG/portal/task rows).
- Fails the build on any red row.
- Runs on PRs to `main` once the GitHub remote exists.

name: Elk OS — KB ingestion step (manifest load into the bundled RAG engine)
description: Flagged by the P3 build agent: the engine boots green but answers nothing until a KB manifest is ingested. Filed per CLAUDE.md §1 rather than left floating. Distinct from P3 (engine integration) — this is the content load.
acceptance_criteria:
- A Claude-free (cron-safe) manifest-ingest step populates the bundled RAG engine so `/query` returns substantive results (currently `/health` is green but the KB is empty).
- Wire it into the CLI (e.g. `elk-os seed` or a dedicated `ingest` subcommand).
- Ship a starter KB manifest for the generic profile (or document how a buyer points it at their own docs); degrade to FTS if local embeddings unavailable.

name: P1 — Compose core: Postgres + Directus up green locally
description:
Stand up the data layer + Directus, non-interactive bootstrap, wired into doctor.

**DONE & verified green 2026-06-29.** `compose.yaml` (postgres:15-alpine + directus/directus:11.5.1) + dev/prod overrides + Caddy template. `elk-os init` generates `.env`+secrets safely (no leak); `up` boots Postgres+Directus and mints the admin static token non-interactively (idempotent); `doctor` really checks Docker/Postgres/Directus `/server/health`/token-auth and gates (all 5 green, exit 0; red+exit1 when down). Resumable via `.elk-os-state.json`. Postgres host port isolated to 15432 (collision-safe). Commits 82f23d0..267cbe3. Prod/Caddy path config-validated but not yet boot-tested against a real domain — that lands with P6 box e2e.
acceptance_criteria:
- `compose/compose.yaml` brings up Postgres + Directus on isolated ports.
- `elk-os up` then `elk-os doctor` reports Directus `/server/health` = ok.
- Admin user + static token bootstrapped non-interactively (no manual UI step, unlike the agencyos-test prototype).
- Idempotent re-run (resumable state file).
- Extends `~/Desktop/DevProd/{setup,apply}-agencyos-test.sh` rather than duplicating.

name: P2 — Directus os_* schema snapshot + profile seed (generic Demo Co / AE)
description:
The os_* shared-state bus schema + profile-switched seed.

**DONE & verified green BOTH profiles 2026-06-29 (built via Max Mode workflow — 8 agents).** Snapshot live prod schema -> pruned 141->50 collections (zero dangling refs) -> scrub-audit (6 cosmetic AnalogElk refs scrubbed) -> 3 adversarial leak-verifiers all leakProven=false -> independent grep clean. `migrate` applies via schema diff+apply (version-aligned, idempotent); `seed` REST-upserts by natural key. Clean-install loop-proof: generic created=17/0, analogelk created=16/0, doctor ALL GREEN, seeded os_task read back via API. Loop-proof caught 3 real bugs (fixed): RAG port 9100 collision->19100, releases.repository_id required, AE seed bare-naming vs kept 'projects' folder. Commits de7fb05, 5222976.
acceptance_criteria:
**Schema source DECIDED 2026-06-29: snapshot live + prune + audit.**
- Take a schema-only `directus schema snapshot` of the live prod Directus (cms.analogelk.com) — real, battle-tested.
- Prune AE-specific collections that don't belong in a generic agency-OS.
- MANDATORY audit: scrub flows / presets / permissions / roles for any real client refs, tokens, emails, or PII before it ships in the generic profile (schema-only carries no row data, so this is the only leak surface).
- `generic` profile seeds synthetic 'Demo Co'; `analogelk` profile seeds AE demo data (also no real secrets/PII).
- `migrate` applies the snapshot to a fresh Directus via `directus schema apply` using DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN; `seed` is profile-switched. Both idempotent.
- `doctor` gains a 'seeded os_task readable' row to prove the human↔agent loop (spec §4.8).
- Watch the `dod_checklist` JSON shape (spec §8) before populating it.

name: Elk OS — installer hardening (from P2 loop-proof findings)
description: Hardening backlog from the P2 loop-proof. None block P2 (worked around), but all are real robustness gaps a sellable installer should close. Filed per CLAUDE.md §1.
acceptance_criteria:
Three real findings surfaced by the P2 from-scratch loop-proof (all worked around, not yet hardened):
1. **`up` resilience:** a non-core overlay (RAG) container failing to start must NOT abort the core Directus bootstrap/token-mint. Reorder so core comes up + bootstraps even if the RAG overlay fails; report the overlay failure as a warning.
2. **`doctor` engine trust:** the RAG health row was fooled by a co-located elk-v3 engine on the same host port (false green). doctor should confirm it's hitting OUR container (check the mapped port/container, or a unique build marker in /health).
3. **seed loader `resolve()`:** should prefer a real table collection over a kept UI-folder alias of the same stem (a bare-named `projects.json` mis-resolved to the `projects` folder). Make it robust to either naming.

name: P5 — Claude-OS wiring (constitution + MCP + hooks + memory; agent reads an os_task)
description:
Install the Claude-side operating system and verify the coordination loop — the heart of the thesis.

**DONE & loop PROVEN 2026-06-29.** Finding: Directus 11.15.1 ships a NATIVE MCP server at /mcp (JSON-RPC streamable-HTTP; items + system-prompt tools), gated by settings.mcp_enabled (default off). `elk-os wire` flips it on and renders, from committed claude-os/ templates into a gitignored per-deployment wire/ dir: `.mcp.json` (elk-os-cms -> http://localhost:8056/mcp, token as ${DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN} env-ref, never literal), profile-switched CLAUDE.md constitution, SessionStart hooks (banner + os_tasks queue), memory seed, run-claude.sh launcher. LOOP PROOF: clean generic boot -> wire read 8 seeded os_tasks back through the wired MCP config; hooks printed '6 open / 8 total'; doctor green incl 'Directus MCP -> tools/list 200' row. Idempotent. No secrets committed/printed. Commits 89a7261, ad27725.
acceptance_criteria:
- `wire` writes CLAUDE.md (profile), `.mcp.json` (pointed at the fresh Directus; token from env, never echoed), hooks, memory seed, skills/commands into the deployment.
- MCP server reachable from a Claude session in the deployment.
- An agent can read a seeded `os_task` from the fresh CMS — proving the human↔agent shared-task loop closes end to end.

name: P7 — Self-host packaging (deploy blueprints, published images, README/landing, release-please)
description:
Turn the working stack into a sellable, one-click self-host product.

**ARTIFACTS DONE & verified locally 2026-06-29.** provision/cloud-init.sh (fresh-Ubuntu one-shot: Docker -> up/migrate/seed/wire/doctor behind Caddy+TLS; portal on apex, Directus on cms.<domain>); deploy blueprints (Render primary one-click + Fly/Railway/Netlify, each honest that the backend must be hosted); GHCR image-publish CI + compose image-or-build toggle (ELK_OS_USE_PUBLISHED_IMAGES); release-please release-type=simple (version.txt 0.1.0 + CHANGELOG); landing-grade README; LICENSE-RECOMMENDATION.md (no binding license committed; recommends source-available BSL/PolyForm) + NOTICE crediting agency-os MIT lineage. Verified: bash -n, YAML/JSON/TOML parse, docker compose config green for box + image + build modes. Commits e2358fc..f63be2f. DEFERRED (honestly not claimed): no live deploy; CI-publish + release-please + clean-room CI doctor gate activate only once a GitHub remote exists -> follow-up filed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Deploy blueprints (render.yaml / railway.json / fly.toml) + 'Deploy' buttons.
- Images published via CI (GH Actions).
- README/landing + LICENSE.
- release-please + Conventional Commits configured per §7 (version owned by the bot).
- Clean-room `up` → `doctor` green in CI from zero.

name: P6 — Cloud deploy + live demo URL
description:
Stand up the showroom — the demo URL.

**LIVE & verified 2026-06-29.** Full stack deployed to EC2 t3.medium (i-02bb458cf55b6cfe9, us-west-2) behind Caddy + Let's Encrypt TLS. Public URLs (independently curl-verified over https from off-box): portal https://34.220.64.149.sslip.io/ + /login -> 200; Directus https://cms.34.220.64.149.sslip.io/server/health -> 200 ok. Pipeline init->up->migrate(17 rows)->seed->wire(native MCP enabled, 8 os_tasks readable via public https API). doctor 7/8 (the 1 red is cosmetic: box-mode portal has no host port so the localhost probe can't see it; portal proven 200 over https). RAG off for demo stability. sslip.io = no DNS needed. Real blocker debugged: portal image was arm64 (Apple Silicon build) vs amd64 box -> qemu binfmt emulation (works, slower; publish amd64 image for durability). Cost ~$0.04/hr running, ~$2/mo stopped.
acceptance_criteria:
- One `generic`-profile instance deployed to a cloud box (Caddy + TLS + domain) or PaaS.
- Publicly reachable demo URL, seeded with synthetic Demo Co.
- `doctor` green remotely.
- Demo URL recorded on the project/epic `feature_url`.

name: Elk OS — live-demo hardening (amd64 portal image, compose RAG gate, doctor box-probe)
description: Real findings from the first live box deploy (P6). The demo is up and serving; these are durability/correctness fixes for a production-grade or persistent demo. Filed per §1.
acceptance_criteria:
Issues surfaced by the P6 live box deploy (all worked around, none blocking the live demo):
1. **amd64 / multi-arch portal image** — the locally-built image was arm64 (Apple Silicon) and runs under qemu emulation on the amd64 box (slower). Build+publish a linux/amd64 (or multi-arch) portal image. THIS ALSO fixes #2.
2. **NEXT_PUBLIC_DIRECTUS_URL baked at build time** — the prebuilt image inlines analogelk.com as the browser-side Directus origin, so deep authenticated browser calls target the wrong host (SSR/login still render 200). Build the portal image with the deployment's public cms origin, or make the browser-side origin runtime-configurable.
3. **compose.images.yaml + RAG-off** — the overlay declares a required RAG_IMAGE / stray rag service even when ELK_OS_WITH_RAG=false, aborting `up` (agent stripped it on-box). Gate the rag block behind WITH_RAG / make RAG_IMAGE optional.
4. **doctor box-mode portal probe** — in box mode the portal has no host port; doctor should probe the public domain via Caddy instead of localhost:3000 (currently a false red).

name: Muster — whitepaper-as-homepage landing (de-AE front door) + redeploy
description: DONE & LIVE 2026-06-29. 17-agent panel (948k tok) -> whitepaper 'The Task Record Is the Coordination Substrate' (docs/whitepaper/) + name RESOLVED to **Muster** ('Muster the agents. Keep the receipts.'; category 'the operating system for agentic software teams'; rejected Elk OS/Cairn/Quorum/Agency OS for dev-namespace collisions + to decouple from Analog Elk). Built a distinctive static landing (site/, bone/pine/ember, editorial serif + monospace Build-Telemetry rail, self-drawing Build-Trail mark) = whitepaper-as-homepage, full paper on /paper.html, inner links to live system. Deployed: Caddy repointed -> apex 34.220.64.149.sslip.io = Muster home, portal demoted to app.<host>, board cms.<host>, all real LE TLS. Verified live (apex title 'Muster...', 200, TLS valid). AE demoted to one origin clause + case-study link. Open polish: name handle-availability check (npm/.dev/github), generic-profile portal so the app.* link isn't AE-branded (task e027c27b).
acceptance_criteria:
- Repurpose the demo front door: a standalone, distinctive static landing branded **Muster** (name chosen by the 17-agent panel; ‘Muster the agents. Keep the receipts.’; category = ‘the operating system for agentic software teams’) that IS the whitepaper-as-homepage, with inner links to the live system.
- De-emphasize Analog Elk (now the case study, not the headline).
- Repoint Caddy on the live box: apex 34.220.64.149.sslip.io = the landing; portal demoted to app.<host>; cms.<host> = board.
- Redeploy + re-screenshot.
- Whitepaper + identity brief committed in docs/whitepaper/.

name: Elk OS — genericize portal branding per profile (generic shouldn't say 'Analog Elk')
description: DONE & LIVE 2026-06-29. Portal rebranded Analog Elk -> MUSTER (M monogram logo + wordmark + titles + footer; tagline 'agency OS that runs itself' kept) via reproducible patches in portal/prepare-context.sh (pinned read-only .build; AE source untouched). amd64 cross-build with correct public origin (NEXT_PUBLIC_DIRECTUS_URL=cms.*), shipped + redeployed; live /login title 'Login | Muster', 0 'analog elk' in bundle. WORKING DEMO LOGIN demo@muster.dev/muster-demo -> employee board (8 tasks); fixed 4 self-host blockers (proxy subdomain redirect, cookie domain, is_test_data visibility, missing directus_users is_test_data/test_only fields). NOTE: demo user is currently ADMIN/editable -> Phase-1 read-only lockdown next. 3 commits.
acceptance_criteria:
- The `generic` profile must rebrand the portal: org name, logo, login tagline, theme — currently the P4 build shows hardcoded 'Analog Elk' branding regardless of profile (only data + schema are profile-switched).
- Drive branding from profile config / env (e.g. NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND_NAME, a logo asset slot, tagline) consumed by the vendored portal, OR a build-time patch in portal/prepare-context.sh per profile.
- analogelk profile keeps AE branding; generic gets neutral 'Your Agency' defaults a buyer overrides.
- Re-screenshot the generic login to confirm.
